title: Psidium sartorianum
intro: Psidium sartorianum, commonly known as the Brazilian guava or guabiroba, is a shrub or small tree species belonging to the Myrtaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The plant is rich in health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Psidium sartorianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments and support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Psidium sartorianum is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants such as myrtle and guava. The Myrtaceae family is further divided into about 130 genera. Within the genus Psidium, there are over 100 species of plants, including P. sartorianum. Psidium sartorianum is native to South America.
